---
title: "Md. Jubayer Hossain's CV"
author:
  # firstname: Md. Jubayer
  lastname: Md. Jubayer Hossain
  position: "Visiting Scholar, Department of Public Health, Daffodil International University"
  address: "Daffodil Smart City, Birulia 1216, Bangladesh"
  contacts:
    - icon: fa phone
      text: "+8801622345906"
      url: "tel:+8801622345906"
    - icon: fa envelope
      text: hossainmj.me@gmail.com
      url: "mailto:hossainmj.me@gmail.com"
    - icon: fa globe
      text: mdjubayerhossain.com
      url: https://mdjubayerhossain.com
format:
  awesomecv-typst:
    text-align: justify
brand:
  typography:
    fonts:
      - family: Poppins
        source: google
        weight: [100, 400, 700]
      - family: Source Sans 3
        source: google
        weight: [100, 400, 700]
        style: [normal, italic]
    base: Source Sans 3
  color:
    primary: "#000000"
    link: "#0563C1"
  defaults:
    awesomecv-typst:
      font-header: Poppins
---

# Research Statement

I am a computational biologist with a track record of building and deploying reproducible multi-omics frameworks to decode the molecular architecture of cancer and neurological diseases. My research addresses three interconnected questions: (1) How can integrative analysis of bulk RNA-seq, single-cell RNA-seq, and spatial transcriptomics identify robust immunological biomarkers and therapeutic targets within tumor microenvironments? (2) Can interpretable machine learning models trained on pan-cancer transcriptomic data reliably stratify patient prognosis and predict immunotherapy response? (3) What computational strategies best characterize dynamic cell-state transitions—such as tumor-associated macrophage reprogramming and microglial activation in neurodegeneration? My published work includes first- and corresponding-author pan-cancer analyses in *Scientific Reports* and *BMC Genomics*, and seven GitHub-hosted reproducible pipelines built on Nextflow, Docker, and nf-core standards. As a GBD Senior Collaborator at IHME, I have contributed to large-scale epidemiological analyses published in *The Lancet*. In my PhD, I aim to develop scalable, interpretable computational methods for cell-state inference and immune landscape modeling, translating multi-omics discoveries into clinically actionable biomarkers and therapeutic hypotheses.


# Education and Training

{{< yaml short_assets/yml/education.yml >}}

# Technical Skills

{{< include short_assets/qmd/skills.qmd >}}

# Research Experience

{{< yaml short_assets/yml/research.yml >}}

# Selected Publications

{{< include short_assets/qmd/publications.qmd >}}

# Papers Under Review

{{< include short_assets/qmd/under-review-papers.qmd >}}

# Research in Progress

{{< include short_assets/qmd/working-papers.qmd >}}

# Selected Conferences

{{< include short_assets/qmd/conferences.qmd >}}

# Professional Recognition

{{< include short_assets/qmd/recognition.qmd >}}

# Teaching Experience

{{< yaml short_assets/yml/teaching.yml >}}

# Leadership Experience

{{< yaml short_assets/yml/leadership.yml >}}

# Community Engagement

{{< yaml short_assets/yml/community.yml >}}

# References

{{< include short_assets/qmd/references.qmd >}}
